name: Algolia API
what_it_does: Hosted search platform providing fast, typo-tolerant full-text search with AI-powered relevance, faceting, and filtering, with an official MCP server for agent-driven search integration.
use_cases: Adding instant search to web/mobile apps with AI relevance via MCP server
Ecommerce product search with faceting and merchandising rules
Indexing and querying structured content from agent workflows
Neural search combining semantic and keyword matching
not_for: Log analytics or time-series search (use Elasticsearch)
Full document storage (Algolia is a search index, not a database)
Teams with very tight budgets at scale
best_when: You need hosted search with excellent developer experience, fast performance, and AI-powered relevance without managing Elasticsearch infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need log analytics, full-text document storage, or have complex aggregation needs.

auth.notes: Admin API key for write operations. Search-only API key for client-side queries. Application ID + API key pair. Multiple key types with different permissions.

pricing.paid_tiers: Grow from $0.50/1k search requests; Premium custom pricing
pricing.notes: Free tier is quite limited for production use. Pricing based on records and search requests. Can become expensive for large catalogs or high-traffic apps.

performance.rate_limits: Search: no hard rate limit; Indexing: 1000 operations/second per index

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent error messages with reason, message, and status fields. Documentation is world-class.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Objects use objectID for idempotent saves. Batch indexing can specify IDs. Partial updates are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Admin API key must never be exposed client-side - easy to leak in agent code
Index names are permanent and can't be renamed (only copied) - plan naming carefully
Indexing is asynchronous - taskID must be waited on for consistency
Free tier record limit (10k) is hit quickly with product catalogs
Neural (AI) search features require separate configuration and different billing

security_score_components.security_notes: Application ID + API key pair. Separate Search API key (read-only, safe for frontends) and Admin API key (write access, server-only). Key restrictions by index, IP, referer. SOC2 Type II, GDPR. ACL-based API key permissions.
